details: begin numerical stability analysis of layer_norm_rm
loaded methodology reference; following output schema from section 6

3-pass compute kernel: reduce<SUM,REDUCE_ROW> for mean (Pass 1) and variance (Pass 2 after sub+square), transform_in_place for rsqrt(var+eps), Pass 3 normalize + optional gamma/beta; uses ckl::reduce<> directly with WaitAndPopPerTile (not accumulate_reduce_block as user note suggested)
scaler emitted via prepare_partial_reduce_scalers (or prepare_reduce_scaler) with inv_W; scaler CB always bfloat16
cb_mean/cb_inv_std/cb_centered/cb_gamma_tiles/cb_beta_tiles all created at input_tensor.dtype; no unpack_to_dest_mode set; default ComputeConfigDescriptor() used when user omits compute_kernel_config
key finding: REDUCE_MATMUL_FIDELITY = HiFi4 is HARD-CODED for REDUCE_ROW + SUM/AVG matmul path; overrides user math_fidelity for the dominant reductions
scaler CB contract: always bf16; partial_scaler pattern uses 2 tiles (full+partial); 1/N injected as srcB of matmul ⇒ divide-then-sum
transform_in_place: copy_tile → user lambda → pack back; for layer_norm: add_unary_tile(eps) + rsqrt_tile; eps guard present (configurable default 1e-5)
binary_op_in_place packs back to cb_a every tile; cb_centered round-trips through L1 4-5 times in Pass 3 at input dtype
rsqrt_tile uses APPROX template param = math_approx_mode; default in ComputeConfigDescriptor() is False ⇒ precise rsqrt

key findings: (1) Pass1/Pass2 reductions hardwired to HiFi4 via REDUCE_MATMUL_FIDELITY; (2) divide-then-sum order via 1/W scaler in matmul srcB; (3) two-pass mean+variance (no Welford); (4) fp32_dest_acc_en is configurable but cb_mean/cb_inv_std stay at input dtype with no UnpackToDestFp32 → only partial benefit; (5) partial-scaler correctly handles non-tile-aligned W using logical width; (6) eps guard present and configurable; (7) intermediate CBs all at input_tensor.dtype, never forced to Float32

wrote 8-section numerical stability profile per reference schema: algorithm summary, error inventory (15 entries), accumulation analysis, numerical guards table, math fidelity profile, tile-boundary precision, configuration exposure, 6 key observations
